title: Data Types
beginnerExplanation: Data types define the kind of value Python is working with. Knowing the type helps Python understand what operations make sense.
intermediateExplanation: Adding two numbers makes sense (5 + 5 = 10). Adding two words joins them ("fire" + "ball" = "fireball"). Python needs to know the type to execute operations correctly.

realWorldAnalogy.title: Different Containers
realWorldAnalogy.description: You wouldn't put soup in an envelope or mail a letter in a bowl. Different things require different containers. Similarly, Python uses specific structures (types) for text, whole numbers, and true/false logic.

mentalModel: You wouldn't put soup in an envelope or mail a letter in a bowl. Different things require different containers. Similarly, Python uses specific structures (types) for text, whole numbers, and true/false logic.

debuggingMission.scenario: An adventurer is trying to use Data Types but hit a common pitfall: Forgetting quotes around strings.
debuggingMission.explanation: Make sure to follow the best practices: Be mindful of what type a function returns.

practiceQuestions.question: What data type is the value "15"?
practiceQuestions.explanation: Because it is wrapped in quotes, it is treated as text (a String), even though it looks like a number.
